title: Meet the new engine behind Plaid Income
text: Plaid Income’s new engine improves income classification, adds granular taxonomy, and gives teams more control over how income is calculated.

title: Build personalized finance apps in Replit with Plaid
text: Plaid’s native Replit connector lets anyone build custom finance apps fast. Turn your financial data into AI tools for budgeting, insights, and investing.

title: Product Updates – April 2026
text: Explore Plaid’s April 2026 updates: new User APIs, margin balances in Investments, automated partner alerts, and smarter payment rules.

title: [New Report] How AI is shifting customer expectations for fintech products
text: AI is reshaping finance. Plaid’s new research shows what consumers expect from intelligent finance and how companies can meet those expectations.

title: Plaid and Perplexity expand integration to power personalized financial insights
text: Plaid and Perplexity expand integration to connect more accounts, helping users track spending, monitor net worth, & plan with AI-powered tools.

title: Building a transaction foundation model to power intelligent finance
text: Learn how Plaid’s transaction foundation model powers intelligent finance with richer context, better categorization, and scalable financial insights.

title: Onboarding Without Compromises: Adaptive flows that stop fraud and drive conversion
text: Onboarding sets the foundation for long-term user engagement. Done right, it instills confidence. Done poorly, it drives customers away.

title: Product Updates - March 2026
text: Plaid's March updates: AI-powered document verification, Home Lending Report in Encompass LOS, and new platform tools for fraud and onboarding.

title: New era of fraud network intelligence: Early results from Plaid Protect
text: See how Plaid Protect uses network intelligence to detect up to 59% more fraud earlier - without adding friction to the user experience.

title: How Plaid powers intelligent finance with Perplexity
text: Perplexity’s new Portfolio delivers an AI-native investment experience powered by Plaid, with secure brokerage linking & real-time portfolio insights.

title: Plaid brings next-gen asset verification solution to Encompass®
text: Plaid’s Home Lending Report now integrates with Encompass to automate mortgage workflows and simplify digital asset verification. Read more.

title: Instant Payments in Europe: From SEPA Compliance to Competitive Advantage
text: Instant payments are live across Europe. New research from Celent, published by Plaid and Clearbank, explores what SEPA Instant means for banks and performance.

title: Building a smarter chatbot
text: How we got our docs-focused AI chatbot to finally understand reference documentation.

title: Product Updates - February 2026
text: Explore February’s updates including API-based bank verification, expanded cash flow insights, new fraud dashboards, and onboarding improvements.

title: Why intelligent finance needs purpose-built AI infrastructure
text: AI is transforming open finance into intelligent finance. Learn how Plaid’s AI infrastructure powers smarter, personalized financial experiences.

title: How to Turn Transaction Intelligence into Measurable Deposit Growth with Atomic
text:

title: Moving from AI Ambition to Action: The Rise of Cognitive Banking
text:
In the current financial landscape, banks are sitting on vast amounts of account holder data and digital behavior. However, the industry faces a fundamental challenge: transactional data and digital behavior remain fragmented across various business lines and channels. This fragmentation limits a bank’s ability to leverage information in timely, relevant, or effective ways. While many institutions are investing […]

title: 2025 HMDA Data on Mortgage Lending Now Available
text: The 2025 HMDA Loan Application Register data can be found on the FFIEC's HMDA Platform.

title: Estimating the Cost of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to Consumers
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau and the Department of Justice Withdraw Joint Statement on Fair Lending and Credit Opportunities for Noncitizen Borrowers
text: CFPB and Justice have withdrawn a joint statement linked to a creditor’s consideration of an individual’s immigration status under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act.

title: Agencies Announce Dollar Thresholds for Applicability of Truth in Lending and Consumer Leasing Rules for Consumer Credit and Lease Transactions
text: The CFPB and the FRB are announcing the dollar thresholds used to determine whether certain consumer credit and lease transactions in 2026 are subject to certain protections under Regulation Z (Truth in Lending) and Regulation M (Consumer Leasing).

title: Agencies Announce Dollar Thresholds for Smaller Loan Exemption from Appraisal Requirements for Higher-priced Mortgage Loans
text: The CFPB, FRB, and OCC are announcing that the 2026 threshold for higher-priced mortgage loans that are subject to special appraisal requirements will increase from $33,500 to $34,200.

title: CFPB’s Supervision Division Releases New 'Humility Pledge'
text: Washington, D.C.—Today, the CFPB made changes to how the Supervision Division conducts exams. Previously, under the leadership of Director Chopra and Biden’s Director of Supervision Lorelei Salas, a former Soros activist who was put on leave in February 2025, this division was the weaponized arm of the CFPB.

title: CFPB Notifies Court it Cannot Lawfully Draw Funds from the Federal Reserve
text: Washington, D.C.—Today, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) filed a notice informing the court in NTEU v. Vought that the Department of Justice’s Office of Legal Counsel (OLC) has determined that the Bureau may not legally request funds at this time from the Federal Reserve under Dodd-Frank.

title: CFPB Reaches Settlement with FirstCash, Inc. and Its Subsidiaries for Military Lending Act Violations
text: Today, in the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) November 12, 2021 lawsuit against FirstCash, Inc., and nineteen subsidiaries alleging violations of the Military Lending Act (MLA), the parties reached a settlement and jointly filed a stipulated final judgment and proposed order, which if entered by the court, would resolve the lawsuit.

title: CFPB Amends Wise Order for Remittance Practices
text: The CFPB amended its January 30, 2025 consent order with the international remittance company Wise resolving claims including advertising inaccurate fees and failing to properly disclose exchange rates and other costs.

title: CFPB Announcement Regarding Enforcement Actions Related to Buy Now, Pay Later Loans
text: The CFPB is announcing that it will not prioritize enforcement actions taken on the basis of the Truth in Lending (Regulation Z); Use of Digital User Accounts to Access Buy Now, Pay Later Loans, 89 Fed. Reg. 47,068 (May 31, 2024) (“Buy Now, Pay Later”).

title: CFPB Keeps Its Enforcement and Supervision Resources Focused on Pressing Threats to Consumers
text: The CFPB is announcing that it will not prioritize enforcement or supervision actions with regard to entities that are currently outside the stay imposed under Texas Bankers Association v. CFPB, No. 24-40705 (CA5).

title: CFPB Offers Regulatory Relief From Registration Requirements for Small Loan Providers
text: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau will not prioritize enforcement or supervision actions for entities that do not satisfy future deadlines to submit registration information under the regulation titled "Registry of Nonbank Covered Persons Subject to Certain Agency and Court Orders".

title: 2024 HMDA Data on Mortgage Lending Now Available
text: The published data contain loan-level information filed by financial institutions and modified to protect consumer privacy.

title: CFPB Offers Regulatory Relief for Small Loan Providers
text: The Bureau will keep its enforcement and supervision resources focused on pressing threats to consumers, particularly servicemen and veterans.

title: CFPB Seeks to Vacate Abusive, Unjust Case Against Townstone
Statement on Designation of Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as Acting Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
text: On January 31, 2025, President Trump designated Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent as Acting Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB).

title: CFPB Orders Wise to Pay $2.5 Million for Illegal Remittance Practices
text: The CFPB ordered the international remittance company Wise to pay nearly $2.5 million for a series of illegal actions.

title: CFPB Updates List of Consumer Reporting Companies
text: Today, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) released its annual list of consumer reporting companies.

title: CFPB Finds Servicemembers Pay More in Auto Lending Market
text: The CFPB published a report showing that U.S. servicemembers pay higher costs and face greater financial risks when taking out credit to buy a car.

title: CFPB Letter to Massachusetts State Legislature on Barring Medical Bills on Credit Reports
text: A letter written by Seth Frotman, CFPB General Counsel, to the Representative John J. Lawn, Jr. and the Senator John J. Cronin of the Massachusetts State Legislature.

title: CFPB Letter to South Dakota State Legislature on Barring Medical Bills on Credit Reports
text: A letter written by Seth Frotman, CFPB General Counsel, to Representative Brian Mulder of the South Dakota State Legislature.

title: CFPB Letter to Oregon State Legislature on Barring Medical Bills on Credit Reports
text: A letter written by Seth Frotman, CFPB General Counsel, to Senator Wlnsvey Campos and the Representative Nathan Sosa of the Oregon State Legislature.

title: CFPB Letter to Washington State Legislature on Barring Medical Bills on Credit Reports
text: A letter written by Seth Frotman, CFPB General Counsel, to Senator Marcus Riccelli and Representative Joe Timmons of the Washington State Legislature.

title: CFPB Report Finds Continued Challenges for Households that Rent
text: Today, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) released two reports looking at national rental payment data from September 2021 to November 2024.

title: CFPB Report Finds Cash-Out Mortgage Refinance Borrowers Improve Credit Scores
text: Today, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) published a report about financial outcomes for cash-out refinance mortgage borrowers.

title: As AI Agents Get Involved in Financial Data Sharing, Leading Standards Body Launches Initiative to Stay Ahead
text:
April 14, 2026 Today, the Financial Data Exchange (FDX)—a leading technical standards body representing banks, fintechs, consumer advocates, and others—is launching a new initiative to promote safety and innovation when AI agents are used by consumers and companies to transmit sensitive financial account data.   The need for industry collaboration on this accelerating trend has grown […]

The post As AI Agents Get Involved in Financial Data Sharing, Leading Standards Body Launches Initiative to Stay Ahead  appeared first on Financial Data Exchange.

title: FDX Submits Response to CFPB’s Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking
text:
Financial Data Exchange (FDX) has submitted a comment letter to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) in response to its Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANPR) for the Personal Financial Data Rights (PFDR) Reconsideration.  In its letter, FDX describes why open, industry-led technical standards are a critical ingredient for a thriving ecosystem that supports innovation, […]

The post FDX Submits Response to CFPB’s Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking appeared first on Financial Data Exchange.

title: FDX Announces Spring 2025 API Release – FDX API Version 6.4
text:
Financial Data Exchange (FDX) has released the Spring 2025 edition of its financial data sharing API, FDX API v6.4. Developed by a diverse membership, this latest release introduces notable technical updates and documentation enhancements that support continued market innovation and interoperability. Version 6.4 incorporates 24 new RFCs and includes: These updates reflect ongoing industry collaboration […]

The post FDX Announces Spring 2025 API Release – FDX API Version 6.4 appeared first on Financial Data Exchange.

title: 114 Million Reasons to Keep Moving Forward on Industry-Led Standard for Secure Data Sharing
text:
SALT LAKE CITY, Utah – April 25, 2025 (GlobalNewsWire) – Financial Data Exchange (FDX) is pleased to report that the use of standardized APIs to enable secure, permissioned data sharing continues to grow. According to a recent survey, roughly 114 million customer connections are now happening through APIs aligned to the FDX standard. This represents a remarkable […]

The post 114 Million Reasons to Keep Moving Forward on Industry-Led Standard for Secure Data Sharing appeared first on Financial Data Exchange.

title: The Clearing House SVP of Product Strategy Ben Isaacson Named Co-Chair of Financial Data Exchange
text:
SALT LAKE CITY, Utah – March 19, 2025 (GlobalNewswire)– Financial Data Exchange (FDX), a CFPB-recognized standard-setting body for Open Banking in the U.S., recently announced that Ben Isaacson, Senior Vice President of Product Strategy at The Clearing House, has been appointed as FDX Board Co-Chair. Isaacson has been a member of the FDX Board since 2019 […]

The post The Clearing House SVP of Product Strategy Ben Isaacson Named Co-Chair of Financial Data Exchange appeared first on Financial Data Exchange.

title: FDX Recognized by CFPB as a Standard-Setting Body, a Step Forward for Open Banking
text:
Salt Lake City, Utah – January 9, 2025 (GlobalNewswire) – The Financial Data Exchange (FDX) is pleased to announce its official recognition by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) as an industry standard-setting body for the U.S. open banking ecosystem. “This recognition provides helpful clarity to the market at a critical time. Many firms are […]

The post FDX Recognized by CFPB as a Standard-Setting Body, a Step Forward for Open Banking appeared first on Financial Data Exchange.

title: MX Chief Advocacy Officer Jane Barratt Named Financial Data Exchange (FDX) Co-Chair
text:
Jane Barratt replaces Steve Smith who has served as FDX Co-Chair since its inception SALT LAKE CITY, Utah – October 22, 2024 (GlobalNewswire) – The Financial Data Exchange (FDX) – an industry standards body focused on Open Banking – today announced MX Chief Advocacy Officer Jane Barratt as its new Board Co-Chair alongside Franklin Garrigues […]

title: FDX Hits 94 Million Accounts, CFPB Publishes FDX’s Standard-Setting Application
text:
Financial Data Exchange (FDX) is proud to report that 94 million consumer accounts are now actively using the FDX API for secure, permissioned data sharing, as highlighted in our Fall Adoption Metrics Survey. This milestone represents an 18 million account increase since March 2024, underscoring the growing trust and adoption of standardized financial data-sharing practices […]

title: How Block built a $200 billion credit operation by seeing customers traditional lenders can’t

text:

title: Fintech Rundown: A Rapid Review of Weekly News
text:

title: Lazard to buy private markets adviser Campbell Lutyens for $575M
text: An additional consideration of up to $85 million will be determined by defined performance criteria over multiple years, Lazard said.

title: Indiana credit union to buy Ohio bank
text: Interra Credit Union’s purchase of The Hicksville Bank – the fourth whole-bank acquisition by a credit union announced this year – would expand the credit union’s footprint into northeast Indiana and northwest Ohio.

title: Fintech OppFi to buy Arizona bank for $130M
text: The Chicago-based subprime lender said pairing its online platform with BNC’s national bank charter lets it offer more banking services on a broader scale.

title: Warsh’s Fed chair nomination moves to full Senate
text: The Senate Banking Committee, including once-holdout Sen. Thom Tillis, voted along party lines to keep alive ex-Federal Reserve Gov. Kevin Warsh’s bid to succeed Jerome Powell atop the central bank.

title: Ex-M&T employee alleges discrimination over gender, sexual orientation
text: An ex-vice president and commercial branch manager based in Long Island alleges she was “treated as a secondclass citizen” by her boss for being a gay woman.

title: ‘We are a new banking desert’: Oregon bank group CEO
text: A tax credit, set to take effect in June, aims to stimulate de novo activity in the state, which hasn’t chartered a new bank since 2007.

title: Citi taps former Google exec as CIO
text: Brian Saluzzo began his tenure with Citi in March, joining as the bank rolls out AI and nears completion of a business transformation initiative.

title: Adyen to buy German loyalty platform Talon.One for $876M
text: The deal will let the Dutch fintech connect a shopper’s online and in-store interactions so promotions and pricing can be adjusted midstream.

title: U.S. Bank marketing chief: Brand refresh conveys ‘we’ve arrived’
text: As part of a brand revitalization, the super-regional bank is using jewel tones to project sophistication and harnessing its shield design in novel ways, CMO Michael Lacorazza said.

title: Mercury nabs conditional OCC charter
text: The fintech has received conditional approval four months after it applied for a national bank charter, when it named SoFi alum Jon Auxier as CEO of the proposed Mercury Bank.
